summary: End-to-end backend + frontend testing of MarcherBien Dropship complete. All 25 pytest backend tests PASS. All requested frontend flows work: login, dashboard, catalog (search/filter/detail drawer with strategy switcher), suppliers CRUD via cards+modal, pricing rules (simulator + apply-all), CSV import wizard (upload → mapping → confirm → history), orders (bulk-fulfill + tracking), WooCommerce sync buttons present, notifications mark-all-read. No console errors, no broken flows.

minor.issue: Non-existent orderIds silently return status='not_found' inside results with overall success:true — consider surfacing partial-failure counter to caller

MongoDB unique index on sku is sparse but non-null duplicate SKUs from catalog import could raise 500; current import builds internal SKU as `{supplierId[:6]}-{sup_sku}` which mitigates collisions but still worth try/except

Calls woo_sync_product with `current=` kw but Depends() token is required and not injected — will fail if invoked. Endpoint itself is protected by Depends so first-level auth OK; however internal call bypasses the dependency by passing `current` (unused). Works today because sync-product ignores current; but the noqa comment shows fragility.

issues: Revenue chart shows only 1 datapoint since all seeded orders have the same createdAt; not a bug but chart visually sparse

action_items: Optional: harden /api/orders/bulk-fulfill to return partial-failure counts
Optional: refactor /api/woocommerce/sync-all internal call (avoid passing fake `current=` and go through repository function instead)
Cosmetic: seed a few orders with different createdAt to make revenue chart more meaningful
critical_code_review_comments: server.py is ~830 lines — consider splitting into routers (auth, suppliers, products, orders, wc) for maintainability
All routes use Depends(get_current_user) — good auth coverage

Pydantic models used for request bodies where appropriate (LoginResponse, TrackingPayload, BulkFulfillPayload); a few endpoints still accept raw dict (update_supplier/update_product/create_order) — consider Pydantic partial models
compute_retail_price + auto_select_supplier verified: cheapest → 0.38€ Pedibaby, fastest → Bébé Distribution (2-4d), most_stock → Santé Bébé (500u) — matches spec
Catalog import auto-suggest mapping correctly maps sku→supplierSku, name→name, price→costPrice, stock→stock

All UI pages have data-testid on critical selectors (login, metrics-grid, products-table, orders-table, strategy-*, tracking-input, save-tracking, mapping-*, confirm-import, sync-*, notif-*, mark-all-read)
seed_data_creation: Test suite creates a few TEST_ prefixed suppliers/pricing-rules that are cleaned up in same run; CSV import creates 2 QATEST-100/101 products that remain (idempotent — safe to re-run)
context_for_next_testing_agent: Everything works. Backend regression suite at /app/backend/tests/backend_test.py — run `pytest /app/backend/tests/ -v`. Uses REACT_APP_BACKEND_URL from /app/frontend/.env. WooCommerce sync to real store returns success:true or a network error — either acceptable per PRD. Bulk-fulfill mutates seeded pending order MB-2026-0001 to processing then shipped after tracking test; if you need a fresh dataset re-run `python /app/backend/seed.py`.
rca of the issue: No issues found — all APIs return correct data, JWT auth enforced, multi-supplier auto-selection strategies verified numerically, catalog import round-trips CSV → mapping → DB, and every UI page renders with functional data-testid selectors.
